title: Composite unique with explicit name
prompt: Build a Membership schema: a User may be member of many Organizations, but each (user, organization) pair should appear at most once. Write schema + migration. The unique constraint should be on (user_id, organization_id) jointly. Use explicit `name: :memberships_user_id_organization_id_index` on BOTH the migration's unique_index AND the changeset's unique_constraint(:user_id, name: ...) to avoid Postgres name-inference mismatches. Write schema to lib/my_app/accounts/membership.ex, migration to priv/repo/migrations/20260412500000_create_memberships.exs.
tier_rationale: Requires using explicit name: option to avoid Postgres 63-char truncation + name-inference bug documented in Ecto #3259.
research_citation: Ecto Issue #3259: 'When an index name is bigger than 63 characters, PostgreSQL truncates the name.'
